article_meta.title: Metro An open letter from the heart of Iran to America – Don’t give up on us By California Post Staff Published March 7, 2026, 7:11 p.m

reasoning: The summary adopts some of the article’s loaded framing and presents contested claims (notably the reported deaths of Khamenei and Ahmadinejad) as facts, with limited attribution. Language like “repressive” and “brutal crackdowns” appears in the model’s voice. It is not uniformly sensational, but neutrality and attribution are insufficient. The sources_represented field also misidentifies the perspective.

custom_reasoning.spin: Uses interpretive descriptors like “repressive” and “brutal” rather than strictly neutral wording.
custom_reasoning.unsubstantiated_claims: States that Ahmadinejad and Khamenei were killed as if confirmed, rather than attributing as the author’s claim.
custom_reasoning.opinion_as_fact: Generalizes about Iranians’ views and labels the regime without clear attribution.
custom_reasoning.sensationalism: Elevated tone with phrases like “desperate hope” and emphasis on deaths, but not overly dramatic.
custom_reasoning.mudslinging: No direct personal insults from the model; criticism is mostly about the regime.
custom_reasoning.mind_reading: Asserts sentiments of “general joy” and collective desires of Iranians without evidence.
custom_reasoning.slant: Reflects the single-perspective nature of the source; does not add new imbalance.
custom_reasoning.bias_by_omission: Captures the main perspective; no major opposing views existed in the source to omit.
custom_reasoning.word_choice: Employs politically charged terms like “regime” and evaluative adjectives.
custom_reasoning.negativity_bias: Negative elements mirror the source; not significantly amplified.
custom_reasoning.elite_populist_bias: No clear privileging of elite or grassroots beyond the source content.
custom_reasoning.framing_neutrality: Does not sufficiently distance itself from the article’s framing; adopts loaded terms.
custom_reasoning.perspective_completeness: Mislabels sources_represented as 'New York Post' and omits the author’s viewpoint as a distinct perspective.
